rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Khan and Dhar (2006) in its theoretical contribution section to anchor prior evidence that goal-consistent virtuous choices can license later indulgence. It then positions its own contribution relative to that work by arguing that its vice-virtue calorie averaging bias is a cognitive process that can act as an antecedent to motivational licensing effects. Because this citation is used for conceptual positioning and mechanism contrast (not just a background list), it is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Khan and Dhar (2006) as prior licensing evidence, then situates its own theory as a distinct cognitive antecedent to such licensing outcomes. It uses the cited work to frame how its calorie-estimation bias connects to, but is different from, motivational licensing processes.
